mapping.a_element: 3D bubbles in GFT Feynman diagrams (sites of divergences)
mapping.b_element: bubbles in the 2-complex (sites of divergences in lattice topological field theories)

mapping.a_element: 2D triangulations characterizing the boundary of 3D bubbles
power counting of divergences in the Boulatov model
mapping.b_element: divergence degree given by the second Betti number of the 2-complex

mapping.a_element: special class of graphs with complete contraction procedure
mapping.b_element: special cases (simply connected 2-complex or Abelian structure group)

brief_justification: Both papers address the origin and quantification of divergences in discrete quantum gravity models, where divergences arise from topological structures (3D bubbles in GFT vs. bubbles in 2-complexes). The power counting in A and the divergence degree in B are both determined by topological invariants (boundary triangulations vs. Betti numbers), and both restrict to special cases where the machinery simplifies.
